task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the governance compliance specification file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the contents of 'resource_governance_spec.yaml' open in a text editor (like gedit or nano) or displayed in the terminal via a command like 'cat'.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses and logs into the Rancher web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a web browser navigated to https://localhost with the Rancher login screen (credentials entered) or the main Rancher dashboard indicating a successful login as 'admin'.

task_completion.description: Agent implements ResourceQuota and LimitRange for the fintech-dev namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Rancher UI displaying created ResourceQuota and LimitRange objects within the 'fintech-dev' namespace, or a terminal showing successful 'kubectl apply' commands for these objects in the 'fintech-dev' namespace.

task_completion.description: Agent implements ResourceQuota and LimitRange for the fintech-prod namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Rancher UI displaying created ResourceQuota and LimitRange objects within the 'fintech-prod' namespace, or a terminal showing successful 'kubectl apply' commands for these objects in the 'fintech-prod' namespace.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacts with the Rancher UI or Kubernetes terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The web browser shows the actual Rancher application interface with interactive elements, or the terminal shows genuine kubectl command execution, rather than a mocked text file or static HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent does not manually type out fake success outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal outputs or UI confirmation messages appear as standard system responses, without evidence of the agent echoing or printing fake 'created' strings.

integrity.description: Agent uses the provided specification file without altering its requirements to make the task easier.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not edit the original 'resource_governance_spec.yaml' to remove constraints or lower limits before applying it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target namespaces that require governance policies are exactly 'fintech-prod', 'fintech-staging', and 'fintech-dev'. The specification file containing the exact parameters to apply is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/resource_governance_spec.yaml'.
